name: M9 minimal medium + fumarate (2 g/l), post glucose-to-fumarate shift (PMC5043093)
description: M9 minimal medium + fumarate (2 g/l), post glucose-to-fumarate shift — medium reported in PMC5043093 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC5043093 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Complex medium: chemically-defined portion mapped to BiGG; biological extracts listed in `unmapped`. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.base_expansion: Base 'M9' expanded to standard composition: M9 minimal medium (Sambrook & Russell 2001): Na2HPO4, KH2PO4, NaCl, NH4Cl, MgSO4, CaCl2 + carbon source.
provenance.verification_evidence: 'M9 minimal medium, which was prepared as previously described (Kotte et al, 2014)' (main_text); expanded to the standard M9-salts composition since neither the paper nor Kotte 2014 restate the phosphate concentrations.
provenance.base_medium: M9 minimal medium (standard Sambrook/CSH M9 salts) with (NH4)2SO4 as N source and thiamine-HCl as sole vitamin, per Kotte et al 2014

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as 'prepared as previously described (Kotte et al, 2014)' = Mol Syst Biol 10:736, PMID 24987115, PMC4299493
standard M9-salts composition (phosphates, NaCl, MgSO4, CaCl2) since neither paper restates them
sources_chased.how: WebSearch/WebFetch canonical M9 (Sambrook / CSH Protocols M9 salts 5X)

missing_before: N source is (NH4)2SO4 (adds sulfate), not NH4Cl
recuration.notes: Aerobic shake-flask cultivation (50 ml in 500-ml flask, 37C, 300 rpm). The base M9 is standard Sambrook/CSH salts; the two documented modifications from Kotte 2014 are (NH4)2SO4 as N source and thiamine-HCl (1 mg/L) as the ONLY vitamin. IMPORTANT: standard M9 contains NO trace-element/micronutrient solution, so the Fe2+/Fe3+, Mn2+, Zn2+, Cu2+, Co2+, molybdate and Ni2+ exchanges in the current model list are genome-scale-model default open exchanges, NOT defined medium constituents; they are not supported by any cited recipe and should not be treated as a recipe trace mix.
